## [00:00 - 03:01]

The interview flow process, this is essentially going to be the first step before you actually hop onto the interview with any sort of prospect is actually looking out and checking out their application before the interview. So this is pretty important. One of the biggest things with this is just to make sure that you actually read through it for any red flags that you may need to dive into a little bit deeper on the actual interview. And there are a couple of things, these are kind of the main ones here. So their recent experience, the familiarity with the online space, their full-time availability, if they can go full-time and how long it will take them to go full-time. And I'll kind of explain this a little bit more later on, but with where we're at right now, we probably get 800 applications. This is April 17th, we're getting about 800 applications a month. So there's a lot of people coming through. So if they're not available to go full-time, want to dig into that super, super deeply. And if they're just looking for something on the side on top of what they're already doing right now, then they're going to be a DQ, but obviously we're going to talk about that later on. And then as well, just finding out where they found out about us, because this is super important for tracking. So I'm going to go into my calendar. This is just one person here, for example. We'll take, let's see, Kevin here spoke with him yesterday. So essentially before the interview, what you'll do is you'll come into your calendar, obviously here, grab their name, copy and paste it, and then come into Asana, you'll have access to this, and then here you'll click on their name. Sometimes what will happen is you'll put their name in here and just nothing won't show up. So there is one person, I believe, this person here actually. So if we go, if you put an eight Escobar, nothing comes up. But if you then just search, how to search Escobar, it's Nathaniel here, essentially. So some people will use different names on their application versus their calendar. One other thing you can do is also just take their email, whatever they use to submit the calendar and the calendar. Just take that email, copy and paste it, put it here under the search bar in Asana, or their phone number, or just start searching their first name or last name. At some point we'll find that it's super, super rare. This doesn't really happen where their application isn't there. So let's just take, go back to Kevin here really quickly. So let's just take Kevin. Really just want to go through the application and essentially just see where they're at, how many hours they're able to take, what they're currently doing right now. So if the application is not employed, that's something you want to make sure you dive into super deeply on the actual interview. With this specific person, I remember he had a, he actually did have a part-time job as a recruiter, did insurance sales in the past.

## [03:01 - 03:58]

And then from there, just kind of why they're looking for a new opportunity. This really just gives you a little bit of an overview as to where they're at, what they're looking for. And then as well as their familiarity with the online space, what that looks like. And then really from there, when they can start, they can start now and then from here, where they actually found out about us. So some people will say Facebook, some people will say YouTube, some people will say Cole or Savannah, that's obviously going to be organic. This is super, super important because this is going to go into the tracking section. So make sure you actually dive super deeply into where they found out about us. So they say Facebook, it could be a Facebook group, it could be a Facebook post. So make sure you're super, super specific whether it's a Facebook post or a Facebook group, just make sure you ask them that before you actually go into the interview. But other than that, pretty straightforward, just make sure you have a look through your applications, see if there's any red flags that come up. So at that point, you can then address those red flags on the actual interview when you talk with them.
